userStories.title: Project scaffolding — Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 + KV
userStories.description: As Ralph, I need to scaffold the Nexus project from scratch: Cloudflare Workers with Hono framework, D1 database, KV namespace, wrangler.toml configuration, TypeScript setup, and a working 'hello world' health check endpoint. This is the foundation every subsequent story builds on.
acceptanceCriteria: wrangler.toml configured with D1 binding (NEXUS_DB), KV binding (NEXUS_KV), and compatibility_date
Hono installed and serving GET /health returning {status: 'ok', version: '0.1.0'}
TypeScript compiles without errors (tsc --noEmit passes)
package.json with scripts: dev (wrangler dev), deploy (wrangler deploy), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), test
.dev.vars.example documents required secrets: RESEND_API_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
wrangler dev starts without errors

userStories.title: Database schema — agents, traces, spans, users, api_keys
userStories.description: As Ralph, I need to define the D1 database schema with migrations for all core entities: users, api_keys, agents, traces, spans, and subscriptions. The schema must support multi-tenancy, plan limits, and efficient trace queries.
acceptanceCriteria: migrations/0001_schema.sql creates: users (id, email, created_at, stripe_customer_id, plan), api_keys (id, user_id, key_hash, name, created_at, last_used_at), agents (id, user_id, name, description, created_at), traces (id, agent_id, user_id, name, status, started_at, ended_at, metadata), spans (id, trace_id, parent_span_id, name, status, started_at, ended_at, input, output, error), subscriptions (id, user_id, stripe_subscription_id, plan, status, current_period_end)
All tables have IF NOT EXISTS guards
Indexes on: traces(agent_id, started_at DESC), spans(trace_id), api_keys(key_hash)
Migration runs cleanly via wrangler d1 execute

userStories.title: Landing page — 'Plausible for AI agents' positioning
userStories.description: As Ralph, I need to create the public landing page at GET / with clear 'Plausible for AI agents' positioning, pricing table (Free vs Pro $9/mo), feature list, and CTAs. Server-rendered HTML with Tailwind CDN. No JavaScript frameworks.
acceptanceCriteria: GET / returns HTML with headline, subheadline, and 3 key benefits
Pricing table shows Free (1K traces, 1 agent, $0) and Pro (50K traces, unlimited agents, $9/mo) tiers
Feature comparison table: trace viewer, alerts, team access, retention, support
Two CTAs: 'Start free' (→ /register) and 'View demo' (→ /demo or live trace)
Meta-narrative section: 'Built by an AI agent (Ralph), for AI agents'
Mobile-responsive at 375px width — no horizontal overflow
GET /health still returns {status: 'ok'}

userStories.title: Auth — magic link email login, session cookies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to register and log in with my email address via magic link (no passwords). Sessions are stored in KV with a 7-day TTL. Rate limiting prevents abuse.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/register accepts email, sends magic link via Resend API, stores pending token in KV (TTL 15min)
GET /auth/verify?token=... validates token, creates user in D1 if new, sets session cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax), redirects to /dashboard
POST /auth/logout clears session cookie and deletes KV entry
Rate limit: max 5 magic links per email per hour (KV-backed)
GET /dashboard redirects to /auth/login if no valid session
Auth middleware function usable as Hono middleware on protected routes

userStories.title: API key management — create, list, revoke
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create API keys that my agents use to send traces. I need to be able to list my active keys and revoke compromised ones. Keys are shown once at creation and stored as SHA-256 hashes.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /dashboard/keys lists all API keys for the authenticated user (masked: show first 8 chars + ...)
POST /dashboard/keys creates a new key: generates 32-byte random key, stores SHA-256 hash in D1, returns plaintext key ONCE in response
DELETE /dashboard/keys/:id soft-deletes key (sets deleted_at)
API key auth middleware: extracts Bearer token from Authorization header, hashes it, looks up in D1, attaches user_id to request context
Invalid or revoked keys return 401 {error: 'Invalid API key'}

userStories.title: Trace ingestion API — POST /api/v1/traces
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a simple API endpoint where my agent can POST trace data. The endpoint validates the API key, checks plan limits, and stores the trace in D1. Design is OTEL-inspired but simpler.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/traces accepts: {agent_id, name, status ('running'|'success'|'error'|'timeout'), started_at, ended_at?, metadata?}
Returns {trace_id: '<uuid>'} on success (201)
Validates API key via Authorization: Bearer <key> header
Enforces plan limits: Free users capped at 1,000 traces/month (count from D1, cache in KV). Returns 429 {error: 'Monthly trace limit reached. Upgrade to Pro.'} when exceeded
Creates agent record in D1 if agent_id is new for this user
Input validation: required fields, status enum, started_at is ISO 8601
Returns 400 with field-level errors for invalid input

userStories.title: Span ingestion — POST /api/v1/traces/:id/spans
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add spans (sub-steps) to a trace. Spans capture individual LLM calls, tool uses, or sub-agent invocations with timing, input, output, and error data.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/traces/:id/spans accepts: {name, parent_span_id?, status ('ok'|'error'), started_at, ended_at?, input?, output?, error?}
Returns {span_id: '<uuid>'} on success (201)
Validates trace_id belongs to the authenticated user's API key
input/output fields are stored as JSON strings (arbitrary object)
error field is stored as string
Returns 404 if trace not found or not owned by caller
No separate plan limit check (spans are included in trace cost)

userStories.title: TypeScript SDK — npm package for agent instrumentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a simple TypeScript SDK to instrument my agents without writing raw HTTP calls. The SDK wraps trace and span creation with a fluent interface. It must be open-source.
acceptanceCriteria: sdk/ directory with package.json (name: @keylightdigital/nexus, private: false), tsconfig.json, src/index.ts
NexusClient class: constructor({apiKey, baseUrl, agentId})
client.startTrace({name, metadata?}) returns a Trace object with trace_id
trace.addSpan({name, input?, output?, error?}) returns a Span object with timing auto-captured
trace.end({status}) finalizes the trace
All methods handle network errors gracefully (log to console, never throw)
README.md in sdk/ with 10-line quickstart example
TypeScript types exported: NexusClient, TraceOptions, SpanOptions, TraceStatus

userStories.title: Trace viewer — browse traces, expand spans
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to browse my traces in a web UI, see each trace's status and duration, and expand a trace to see all its spans in a waterfall view.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /dashboard/traces lists recent traces for the authenticated user: trace name, agent name, status (color-coded), duration, started_at. Paginated (20 per page).
GET /dashboard/traces/:id shows trace detail: all spans in waterfall order (started_at ASC), each span shows name, status, duration, input/output/error (collapsible)
Status colors: success=green, error=red, running=yellow, timeout=orange
Duration formatted as: <1s shows ms, >=1s shows seconds with 2 decimal places
Empty state: 'No traces yet. Add the SDK to your agent to start tracking.' with link to docs
Mobile-responsive

userStories.title: Dashboard — agent health overview and trace volume chart
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a dashboard homepage that shows a health summary across all my agents: total traces this month, error rate, average latency, and a 7-day trace volume sparkline.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /dashboard shows: traces this month (with plan limit), error rate %, avg duration (ms), agent count
Per-agent health cards: agent name, last trace status, last trace time, 24h error rate
7-day trace volume: daily counts as a simple bar chart (inline SVG or CSS bars, no JS charting library)
Plan usage bar: 'X of 1,000 traces used this month' (Free) or 'X of 50,000' (Pro)
Quick link: 'View all traces' and 'Create API key'
All metrics computed from D1 queries (no caching required for MVP)

userStories.title: Email alerts — notify on agent failure
userStories.description: As a Pro developer, I need email alerts when an agent trace ends with status 'error' or 'timeout'. Alerts are sent via Resend and include the trace name, error message, and a link to the trace viewer.
acceptanceCriteria: Alert triggers on trace ingestion when status is 'error' or 'timeout' AND user is on Pro plan
Alert email sent via Resend API: from=alerts@nexus.keylightdigital.com, to=user's email
Email subject: 'Agent alert: [agent_name] trace failed'
Email body: agent name, trace name, status, error message (from trace metadata or error span), link to /dashboard/traces/:id
Rate limit alerts: max 1 alert per agent per 5 minutes (KV-backed, TTL 300s) to prevent alert fatigue
Free users: no alerts (returns without sending). Pro check via subscriptions table.

userStories.title: Multi-agent management — agent list and per-agent trace history
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to see all my registered agents, their health status, and navigate to per-agent trace history.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /dashboard/agents lists all agents for the user: name, created_at, last trace status, last trace time, 24h trace count
GET /dashboard/agents/:id shows per-agent trace list (same as /dashboard/traces but filtered)
Agents are auto-created on first trace ingestion (no manual creation needed)
Agent name shown in breadcrumbs on trace detail page
Free tier: if user has >1 agent (only allowed on Pro), show upgrade prompt on /dashboard/agents

userStories.title: Stripe billing — Free/Pro Checkout and webhook handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to upgrade from Free to Pro via Stripe Checkout. The system must handle subscription events via webhook to keep the user's plan current.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /dashboard/billing shows current plan, usage, and 'Upgrade to Pro' button (Free) or 'Manage billing' (Pro)
POST /dashboard/billing/checkout creates Stripe Checkout session (mode=subscription, price=Pro $9/mo) and redirects to Stripe
Stripe redirects to /dashboard/billing/success on completion
POST /api/webhooks/stripe handles: customer.subscription.created (set plan=pro), customer.subscription.deleted (set plan=free), customer.subscription.updated
Webhook validates Stripe-Signature header
Stripe customer created on first checkout attempt and stored in users.stripe_customer_id

userStories.title: Plan limits enforcement — hard limits with clear error messages
userStories.description: As Ralph, I need to enforce Free plan limits (1K traces/month, 1 agent) with clear error messages that encourage upgrade — not silent drops or cryptic errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Trace ingestion: count current month traces for user (cache in KV, TTL 1 hour). Return 429 with {error: 'Monthly trace limit reached', limit: 1000, current: X, upgrade_url: '/dashboard/billing'} when Free user exceeds 1K
Agent creation: if Free user already has 1 agent and tries to ingest for a second agent_id, return 403 with {error: 'Free plan limited to 1 agent', upgrade_url: '/dashboard/billing'}
Dashboard shows plan limit banner when usage > 80% of Free limit
Upgrade link in all limit errors points to /dashboard/billing
Pro users have no hard limits (50K traces is a soft guideline, not enforced at MVP)

userStories.title: Settings page — API keys, account info, danger zone
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a settings page where I can manage my API keys, see my account email, and delete my account.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /dashboard/settings shows: account email, plan badge, API keys section (list + create + revoke), danger zone
API key creation inline (POST /dashboard/keys from settings page, same handler as ACP-005)
DELETE /dashboard/account with confirmation text input ('delete my account') — deletes user, all agents, all traces, all spans, all API keys, cancels Stripe subscription if active
Account deletion is irreversible — show warning with data loss summary before confirming
Redirect to / with 'Account deleted' message after deletion

userStories.title: Data retention — auto-delete old traces
userStories.description: As Ralph, I need a scheduled job to delete traces older than the plan retention window (30 days Free, 90 days Pro) to control D1 storage costs.
acceptanceCriteria: Cloudflare Cron Trigger configured in wrangler.toml: runs daily at 02:00 UTC
Scheduled handler deletes traces (and their spans) where started_at < NOW - retention_days
Retention days determined per-user by plan (30 Free, 90 Pro)
Batched deletes: process max 1,000 traces per run to stay within D1 limits
Logs deleted count to console for observability

tagline: Simple, affordable agent observability for indie developers — the Plausible of AI agents
author: Ralph (AI agent, Keylight Digital LLC)

positioning.problem: Developers building AI agents have no simple, affordable way to monitor what their agents are doing. Enterprise tools (Galileo $100/mo, LangSmith $39/mo) are overkill. Self-hosting Langfuse is a maintenance burden. There is no 'Plausible' for AI agents — simple, privacy-aware, just works.
positioning.solution: A hosted agent observability dashboard. Drop in the SDK. See your traces. Get alerts when agents fail. No self-hosting, no enterprise contracts, no lock-in.
positioning.targetCustomer: Indie developers and small teams (1-10 people) building AI agents who need observability without enterprise complexity or cost.
positioning.positioning: Plausible for AI agents — simple, affordable, privacy-aware. $9/mo. No self-hosting. No contracts.
positioning.differentiator: The only hosted agent control plane priced for individual developers. Cloudflare-native means near-zero COGS and global edge performance. Open-source SDK from day 1.
positioning.metaNarrative: Built by an AI agent (Ralph), for AI agents. Keylight Digital built this because we needed it to manage our own agent infrastructure.

architecture.cache: Cloudflare KV (rate limits, sessions, API key lookup)
architecture.frontend: Server-rendered HTML + Tailwind CDN (no build step, proven Beam pattern)
architecture.rationale: Same stack as Beam. Ralph has proven patterns for all components. Zero infrastructure cost on CF free tier.

distributionStrategy.principle: Distribution-first. Launch publicly within the first week, not after 186 stories.
actions: Open-source the SDK on GitHub from day 1 (drives stars, backlinks, trust)
Show HN post within first 7 days of launch — 'Show HN: I built an open-source control plane for AI agents, runs on Cloudflare, $9/mo'
Dev.to and Hashnode articles: 'How I monitor my AI agents for $9/month'
Submit to awesome-ai-agents, awesome-llm-observability within week 1
Build in public on Twitter/X — post trace screenshots, agent failures, weekly metrics
Cloudflare partner ecosystem: submit to Cloudflare Marketplace / Workers templates
distributionStrategy.keyDifferenceFromBeam: Beam launched with zero distribution plan. Nexus pre-writes the Show HN post before the first story is coded. The README IS the launch post.

lessonsFromBeam.summary: 184 stories, 0 external users, $0 revenue. Every lesson points to distribution.
lessons: Distribution must be designed in Story 1, not Story 184
Open-source is a distribution channel, not a risk — Rybbit proved this (11K stars, traction)
SEO collision kills organic discovery — validate the brand name against search results before launch
The awesome-list strategy requires human follow-up; agent can open PRs but can't merge them
Product Hunt within week 1, not after the product is 'finished' — it's never finished
The meta-narrative must be in the README from day 1: 'built by an AI agent for AI agents'
Rate limiting in local dev must skip loopback addresses (::1, 127.0.0.1) — Beam lost hours to this
Playwright smoke tests catch regressions; run them after every deploy, not just at launch

drivers: JetBrains Central EAP launches Q2 2026 — narrows indie first-mover window
EU AI Act general application August 2026 — creates compliance driver even for SMBs
Galileo just went open-source — free tier will absorb power users who self-host
Enterprise tools (Snowflake, Microsoft) are building down-market features
Activant Capital published VC thesis — institutional money will fund competitors within 6-12 months
urgency.verdict: The window is open now. 3-6 months before enterprise trickle-down or open-source hosted tiers close the gap.

description: Simple, affordable agent observability for indie developers — the Plausible of AI agents
